key_insight: AEGIS is a multi-task mammography AI that uses JEPA pre-training and supervised fine-tuning to detect breast cancer and assess breast density in one model.
clinical_relevance: The system may help prioritize suspicious mammograms and support density reporting, with performance approaching strong clinical screening benchmarks in internal and external evaluations.
method: Pre-trained three Vision Transformer sizes (Small/Base/Large) with self-supervised JEPA on 71,103 mammography studies from 14 sites.
Fine-tuned the models for breast cancer triage and BI-RADS density classification using progressive resolution scaling up to 2048×1536.
Evaluated on a curated test set and externally on VinDr-Mammo, including an ensemble with an FDA-cleared baseline.
evidence_signal: On a 785-study test set, the largest model reached 0.949 AUC for cancer triage, 93% sensitivity, 75% specificity, and 0.953 AUC for binary density classification; external zero-shot triage AUC on VinDr-Mammo was 0.871.
watch_item: Performance was measured on curated and external datasets, but real-world deployment would still require prospective validation, workflow integration, and careful review of differences in reference standards and population shift.
paper_title: AEGIS: A Multi-Task Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture for Mammography
